framework.name: Signed Payload Model (Not On-Chain)
framework.description: Verifiable Intent uses signed cryptographic records with selective disclosure — NOT blockchain/on-chain attestation. Protocol-agnostic design working across 'agentic protocols, devices, wallets, platforms, and other payment networks.' Will integrate W3C Verifiable Credentials for portable delegation proof over time.
framework.raw_quote: Over time, Mastercard says Verifiable Intent will be strengthened through integration with Verifiable Credentials, a W3C standard that would make consumer authorization and delegation more explicit, portable, and cryptographically verifiable across systems.

source.title: Mastercard and Google's new trust layer could reshape how AI buys for you
